# Branching — engrava-benchmark

This repository uses single-trunk GitHub Flow: `main` is the only permanent branch,
and every change lands through a PR with green CI. There is no permanent `dev` branch,
no release-trigger branch, and no semantic-release flow.

## Branches

| Branch | Role | Who pushes |
|---|---|---|
| `main` | Stable default branch. All changes land through PRs with green CI. | Maintainers (via PR) |
| `<type>/<kebab-description>` | Short-lived feature, fix, chore, docs, and maintenance branches. | Contributors |
| `results/<kebab-description>` | Short-lived benchmark-result branches. Use `chore(results): ...` for result rows and leaderboard data. | Contributors |
| `feature/<epic>` or `release/v<X.Y.Z>` | Optional temporary staging branches for larger multi-PR work. They are not permanent and do not trigger release automation. | Maintainers |

## Feature / fix / chore branch naming

- Format: `<type>/<kebab-description>`
- Type values: `feature`, `fix`, `chore`, `docs`, `style`, `refactor`, `perf`, `test`, `build`, `ci`, `revert`, `content` (matches Conventional Commits types).
- Description: plain English, kebab-case, ≤ 50 chars total.
- No internal-looking identifiers. No abbreviations that wouldn't make sense to a first-time reader.

### Examples

| Type | Branch | Conventional Commit subject |
|---|---|---|
| Feature | `feature/priority-signal-hybrid-search` | `feat(dreaming): add priority signal to hybrid search` |
| Bug fix | `fix/empty-count-on-mindql` | `fix(mindql): handle empty COUNT result without error` |
| Docs | `docs/quickstart-update` | `docs(quickstart): clarify install steps for fresh venv` |
| Chore | `chore/bump-ruff-to-0.8` | `chore: bump ruff to 0.8` |

## Flow

```
                ┌───────────────────────┐
                │  <type>/<description>  │   short-lived work branches
                │  results/<description> │   benchmark result rows
                └───────────┬───────────┘
                            │  PR + green CI
                            ▼
                ┌───────────────────────┐
                │          main          │   only permanent branch
                └───────────────────────┘

Optional temporary `feature/<epic>` or `release/vX.Y.Z` staging branches may collect
larger multi-PR work, but they are not permanent and do not trigger releases.
```

## Pull request expectations

- PRs target `main`. There is no permanent `dev` branch and no release-trigger branch.
- CI must be green before review.
- Commits in the PR use Conventional Commits format.
- Result-row PRs use `results/<kebab-description>` branches and `chore(results): ...` commit messages.
- Squash-merge by default unless a maintainer requests preserving commit history.
- PR description explains *why* (the diff shows *what*). Keep it concise.

## Releases

This repo does not publish package releases from branch automation. Benchmark result
rows and leaderboard updates are data changes reviewed through normal PRs to `main`;
they are not release events.
